meaning: n.<美口>侦破团伙者,扫荡流氓的执法者
examples: After gangbuster earnings quarter after quarter, search king Google has finally disappointed Wall Street.
And with a gangbuster growth of fantasy sports of 12% annually, apparently more sophisticated, inside information about teams and players is always welcome.
Improved profitability, not gangbuster sales, drove up retail stocks last year.
This is why Kickstarter went like gangbuster those first few years.
